articles.title: Why AI Governance Needs Visible Authority Now
articles.description: The advantage AI will not be won by organizations that deploy the most tools; it will go to organizations that can see clearly, decide wisely, and scale responsibly.
articles.content:
Executive visibility is the missing AI governance layer. But visibility only becomes governance when every AI signal has an owner, a decision right, and a path to action.
In a great jazz ensemble, the danger is rarely that the musicians lack talent.

articles.description: This won’t make the already-controversial AI training endeavor any more popular.
articles.content: Meta has paused use of an AI training program that tracks its own employees' keystrokes and mouse movements. The company has suspended the Model Capability Initiative, not because of workers' understandable displeasure around being (almost) perpetual... [540 chars]

articles.title: AI Lending In The US Needs One Rule, Not Fifty
articles.description: Senate Bill 24-205, was the most ambitious American attempt yet to govern how lenders, insurers and employers use AI to make decisions about people.
articles.content: Colorado has repealed the country’s first comprehensive artificial-intelligence law, weeks before it took effect. The episode is a warning about how not to regulate a fast-moving technology, and an argument for Congress to set one clear national rule... [2645 chars]
